choicesEnglish: Good morning, how can I help you?
Good morning, what would you like?
Good morning, who are you looking for?
Good morning, where are you going?
correctAnswerEnglish: Good morning, how can I help you?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! La frase di apertura standard del farmacista.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il farmacista offre il suo aiuto in modo professionale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The standard opening phrase of the pharmacist.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the pharmacist offers their help professionally.

choicesItalian: Ho un forte mal di gola.
Ho un forte mal di testa.
Ho un forte mal di pancia.
Ho un forte mal di schiena.
choicesEnglish: I have a severe sore throat.
I have a severe stomach ache.
correctAnswerItalian: Ho un forte mal di gola.
correctAnswerEnglish: I have a severe sore throat.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! 'Forte' si usa per indicare l'intensità del dolore.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la cliente lamenta un dolore alla gola.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! 'Forte' is used to indicate the intensity of the pain.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the customer complains of a throat ache.

correctAnswerEnglish: I have trouble swallowing.

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Un sintomo tipico del mal di gola.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, cerchiamo il termine per la difficoltà a mandare giù cibo o saliva.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! A typical symptom of a sore throat.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, we are looking for the term for the difficulty in swallowing food or saliva.

correctAnswerEnglish: What do you recommend?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Si chiede consiglio al farmacista per i farmaci da banco.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stiamo chiedendo un parere professionale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! You ask the pharmacist for advice on over-the-counter drugs.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, we are asking for a professional opinion.

choicesEnglish: Do you also have a fever?
Do you also have a cough?
Do you also have a cold?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you also have a fever?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Il farmacista indaga per capire l'entità del problema.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuole sapere se la temperatura corporea è alta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The pharmacist investigates to understand the extent of the problem.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, they want to know if the body temperature is high.

correctAnswerEnglish: Just a bit of irritation.

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Descrive un fastidio leggero alla gola.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la cliente dice di non avere febbre, ma solo fastidio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! It describes a mild discomfort in the throat.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the customer says she doesn't have a fever, just discomfort.

correctAnswerEnglish: I recommend these lozenges/tablets.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! La risposta professionale alla richiesta di consiglio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il farmacista propone un rimedio in formato solido.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The professional response to the request for advice.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the pharmacist proposes a remedy in solid format.

choicesEnglish: To be dissolved in the mouth.
correctAnswerEnglish: To be dissolved in the mouth.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Molte pastiglie per la gola funzionano così.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica la modalità di assunzione corretta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Many throat lozenges work this way.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates the correct mode of intake.

correctAnswerEnglish: They have an anti-inflammatory action.

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Spiega l'effetto terapeutico del farmaco.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, descrive come il farmaco agisce contro l'irritazione.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! It explains the therapeutic effect of the drug.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it describes how the drug acts against irritation.

choicesItalian: Ne prenda una ogni quattro ore.
correctAnswerItalian: Ne prenda una ogni quattro ore.
correctAnswerEnglish: Take one every four hours.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Indica la frequenza di assunzione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stiamo dando istruzioni sulla posologia.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! It indicates the frequency of intake.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, we are giving instructions on the dosage.

choicesItalian: Va bene se le prendo prima di dormire?
Va bene se le prendo domani?
Va bene se le prendo fuori?
Va bene se le prendo a pranzo?
choicesEnglish: Is it okay if I take them before bed?
Is it okay if I take them tomorrow?
Is it okay if I take them outside?
Is it okay if I take them at lunch?
correctAnswerItalian: Va bene se le prendo prima di dormire?
correctAnswerEnglish: Is it okay if I take them before bed?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedere conferma sull'orario di assunzione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la cliente vuole sapere se può prenderle la sera tardi.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Asking for confirmation on the time of intake.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the customer wants to know if she can take them late in the evening.

correctAnswerEnglish: Yes, certainly.

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Una rassicurazione standard del farmacista.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, conferma la possibilità senza dubbi.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! A standard reassurance from the pharmacist.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it confirms the possibility without doubt.

choicesEnglish: I'd like a box of aspirin.
I'd like a box of cookies.
I'd like a box of flowers.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'd like a box of aspirin.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedere un farmaco da banco comune.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stiamo ordinando un medicinale specifico.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Asking for a common over-the-counter drug.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, we are ordering a specific medicine.

choicesItalian: Preferisce le compresse classiche o quelle effervescenti?
Preferisce le compresse rosse o blu?

correctAnswerEnglish: Do you prefer classic tablets or effervescent ones?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Il farmacista chiede il formato preferito.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, dà la scelta tra pastiglie normali e quelle che si sciolgono in acqua.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The pharmacist asks for the preferred format.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, they give a choice between normal pills and those that dissolve in water.

choicesItalian: Prendo quelle effervescenti con vitamina C.
choicesEnglish: I'll take the effervescent ones with vitamin C.
correctAnswerItalian: Prendo quelle effervescenti con vitamina C.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'll take the effervescent ones with vitamin C.

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Una scelta comune per rafforzare le difese.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la cliente sceglie la versione solubile con aggiunta di vitamine.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! A common choice to strengthen defenses.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the customer chooses the soluble version with added vitamins.

correctAnswerEnglish: That's six euros and fifty.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! La comunicazione del prezzo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il farmacista dice il costo totale del prodotto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Communicating the price.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the pharmacist states the total cost of the product.

correctAnswerEnglish: Would you like anything else?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! La domanda standard per completare la vendita.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiede se il cliente ha bisogno di altre cose.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The standard question to complete the sale.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it asks if the customer needs anything else.

choicesItalian: Mi servirebbe del disinfettante e dei cerotti.
choicesEnglish: I would need some disinfectant and some plasters.
correctAnswerItalian: Mi servirebbe del disinfettante e dei cerotti.
correctAnswerEnglish: I would need some disinfectant and some plasters.

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Prodotti base per il pronto soccorso domestico.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stiamo chiedendo articoli per medicare una ferita.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! Basic products for home first aid.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, we are asking for items to treat a wound.

choicesItalian: Ho questa scottatura solare sulle spalle.
choicesEnglish: I have this sunburn on my shoulders.

correctAnswerEnglish: I have this sunburn on my shoulders.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Mostrare o descrivere il problema per avere aiuto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la cliente indica un'irritazione dovuta al sole.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Showing or describing the problem to get help.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the customer indicates an irritation due to the sun.

choicesEnglish: What can I put on it?
What can I say to it?

correctAnswerEnglish: What can I put on it?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedere quale prodotto applicare sulla pelle.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stiamo cercando un rimedio topico.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Asking which product to apply to the skin.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, we are looking for a topical remedy.

choicesItalian: Le consiglio una crema alla calendula.
correctAnswerItalian: Le consiglio una crema alla calendula.
correctAnswerEnglish: I recommend a calendula cream.

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! La calendula è ottima per lenire la pelle arrossata.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il farmacista propone un rimedio naturale specifico.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! Calendula is great for soothing reddened skin.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the pharmacist proposes a specific natural remedy.

correctAnswerEnglish: They are very refreshing.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una qualità importante per curare una scottatura.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stiamo descrivendo la sensazione di sollievo che danno.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! An important quality for treating a sunburn.

choicesItalian: L'aloe va bene anche per i bambini?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Verificare la sicurezza del prodotto per i più piccoli.

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Verifying the safety of the product for little ones.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the customer asks if it is suitable for pediatric age.

correctAnswerEnglish: There are no contraindications.

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Significa che il prodotto è sicuro e non ha effetti negativi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il farmacista rassicura sulla sicurezza del rimedio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! It means the product is safe and has no negative effects.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the pharmacist reassures about the safety of the remedy.

correctAnswerEnglish: I'll take the aloe gel.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la cliente sceglie il prodotto suggerito.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the customer chooses the suggested product.

choicesEnglish: Do you have the health card?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you have the health card?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Necessaria per lo scontrino parlante.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stiamo chiedendo il documento per la detrazione fiscale.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, we are asking for the document for the tax deduction.

choicesEnglish: Is a prescription needed for this?
correctAnswerEnglish: Is a prescription needed for this?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vogliamo sapere se è un farmaco di libera vendita.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! A fundamental question for many medicines in Italy.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, we want to know if it is an over-the-counter drug.

correctAnswerEnglish: It's an over-the-counter drug.

feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! It means no medical prescription is needed.

correctAnswerEnglish: How many times a day?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedere la frequenza di assunzione del farmaco.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stiamo indagando sulla posologia giornaliera.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Asking the frequency of the drug intake.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, we are investigating the daily dosage.

feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! 'Dopo i pasti' means on a full stomach.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it gives a precise temporal instruction linked to food.

choicesEnglish: Do you have the detailed receipt?

correctAnswerEnglish: It may cause drowsiness.

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A common side effect of some drugs.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it warns the customer that they might get sleepy.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the pharmacist recommends caution in use.

feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A wish for a speedy recovery.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stiamo offrendo un contenitore per gli acquisti.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, we are offering a container for the purchases.

choicesEnglish: I have a very painful sunburn.
